foundations-score-and-ddpm.claim_text: Score-based generative modelling and denoising diffusion probabilistic models (NCSN, DDPM) establish a learnable noise-prediction objective whose reverse process samples high-quality data from a tractable Gaussian prior, giving the field its core modelling vocabulary.

foundations-continuous-time-sde.claim_text: Continuous-time score-based stochastic differential equations unify discrete-time DDPM with score-matching and make implicit, deterministic, and accelerated samplers (DDIM, probability-flow ODE) natural special cases.

foundations-latent-and-guidance.claim_text: Latent-space diffusion (LDM / Stable Diffusion) and classifier-free guidance are the two engineering innovations that made diffusion practical at modern scale — by moving the diffusion process into a perceptual / semantic latent and by enabling strong conditional generation without an explicit classifier.

language-discrete-state-markov.claim_text: Discrete-state diffusion (D3PM, DiffusionLM) defines a forward Markov chain over discrete tokens — uniform / absorbing / Gaussian transition kernels — and trains a reverse denoising model that can generate fluent text without an autoregressive factorisation.

language-score-entropy-masked.claim_text: Score-entropy discrete diffusion (SEDD) and masked diffusion language models (MDLM) replace the divergence-based discrete-diffusion losses with score-entropy / masked-token objectives that scale more reliably and close the perplexity gap with autoregressive baselines.

language-llm-scale-diffusion.claim_text: LLM-scale discrete diffusion models (LLaDA, DiffuLLaMA, BeyondAR) demonstrate that non-autoregressive diffusion can match — and in some regimes exceed — autoregressive LLM performance at billion-parameter scale, while enabling parallel decoding and bidirectional context.

biology-protein-sequence.claim_text: Protein-sequence diffusion models (EvoDiff, DPLM) train discrete diffusion on amino-acid sequences and reach parity with — or exceed — protein language models on inverse-folding, generation, and design tasks while supporting flexible conditioning.
